concepts.site_goal: Position a flagship oceanographic research institute as a credible, data-rich hub for scientists, journalists, and curious public visitors by showcasing labs, principal investigators, peer-reviewed publications, live expedition trackers, open datasets, and institutional news under a single navigable taxonomy.
concepts.description: A dense, institutional oceanographic research site rendered in deep teal (#0E3B45) and bone-white (#F3EEE3) with thin rule lines, scientific serif body type (Source Serif), and a persistent left-sidebar navigation that groups content by discipline (Physical Oceanography, Marine Biology, Geochemistry, Polar Science, Ocean Engineering) above a secondary section list. Pages favor multi-column layouts, small caps section labels, footnoted captions, and inline data visualizations. Maps, bathymetric charts, and citation blocks anchor most templates. The overall vibe reads like a working scientific institution: archival, citation-heavy, and quietly authoritative rather than marketing-led.
concepts.motif: Bathymetric contour lines and isobath shading used as decorative section dividers and behind hero figures; tide-table-style data grids; thin teal hairline rules between every content block.

pages.layout_pattern: Two-column with persistent left sidebar nav; right column stacks an institutional hero, current expeditions ticker, featured publication, news triptych, and a strip of live ocean indicators
sections: institutional hero with mission statement and founding year
currently at sea ticker with vessel names and chief scientists
featured publication of the week with DOI and abstract excerpt
three-column news triptych (feature, press release, field dispatch)
live indicators strip (global mean SST anomaly, ENSO state, Arctic sea ice extent)
discipline grid linking into the five research areas

pages.layout_pattern: Filterable directory grid of lab cards grouped under discipline headings, each card showing lab name, PI, instrument badges, and active project count
sections: discipline filter chips along the top
grouped lab card grids by discipline
cross-lab initiatives callout (e.g. Southern Ocean Carbon Program)

pages.layout_pattern: Long-form lab page with sticky in-page TOC, hero figure of lab work, members roster, active projects with grant numbers, instruments, and recent publications
sections: lab header with PI portrait, contact, and ORCID
active projects with funding agency and grant ID
recent publications list with DOI links

pages.layout_pattern: Searchable PI and staff directory in a tight 4-column card grid with discipline filter sidebar and alphabetical jump rail
sections: search bar with role and discipline filters

pages.layout_pattern: Faceted search results page: filter rail on the left within the discipline sidebar, central reverse-chronological citation list with abstract toggles, and a right-rail of saved searches and export tools
sections: search bar with keyword and DOI input
faceted filters (year, author, lab, journal, has dataset)
citation list with expandable abstracts and BibTeX copy

pages.layout_pattern: Full-bleed map tracker on top half showing all active vessel positions, lower half is a table of legs with status pills and a date scrubber tying map and table together
sections: live fleet map with vessel pins and tracks
active legs table (cruise ID, vessel, chief scientist, region, status)
date scrubber linking map state to table
field dispatches feed from currently deployed teams

pages.layout_pattern: Dataset catalog with left-side discipline nav, top filter bar for variable and platform, and a sortable table with inline sparkline previews and per-row download menus
sections: catalog filters (variable, platform, time range, region bbox)

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial three-column grid with a lead feature, secondary press releases column, and field dispatches column, plus archive filter by year and type
sections: press contact and media kit callout

pages.layout_pattern: Two-column listing with role categories on the left (faculty, postdoc, technical, ship crew, administrative) and a detailed posting list on the right with deadline pills
sections: ship crew and marine operations recruiting

message_intent: convey institutional rigor and a multi-decade research record
make ongoing expeditions feel transparent and trackable in near real time
lower friction for scientists to find collaborators, datasets, and publications
content_model: Lab (slug, discipline, lead PI, members, instruments, active grants, recent pubs)
Person (name, title, ORCID, affiliations, h-index optional, expeditions, pubs)
Publication (DOI, authors, journal, year, abstract, dataset links, BibTeX)
Expedition (cruise ID, vessel, chief scientist, leg dates, region, status, track GeoJSON, daily logs)
Dataset (accession ID, instrument, variables, temporal extent, spatial bbox, license, file formats)
News item (date, type: feature/press release/field dispatch, byline, related labs)
interactions: Left sidebar collapses disciplines into accordion groups with active-section highlighting
Publication search with faceted filters (year range slider, author, lab, journal, has-dataset toggle) and BibTeX copy button
Expedition tracker with Leaflet-style basemap, vessel pin, polyline of completed track, dashed projected route, and a date scrubber
Dataset table sortable by variable, with inline sparkline previews and a 'request access' modal for embargoed sets
Cite-this button on every publication and dataset that opens a formatted citation dropdown (APA / Chicago / BibTeX)
Sticky table-of-contents on long lab pages
asset_needs: Bathymetric SVG patterns for hero backgrounds
PI portrait headshots in consistent muted treatment
Vessel and instrument line illustrations (CTD rosette, ROV, glider)
World map basemap tiles for expedition tracker
Sample data plots: T/S diagrams, time series, depth profiles
concepts.mobile_behavior: Left sidebar collapses into a top-anchored disciplines drawer; multi-column lab and publication layouts reflow to a single column with sticky section jump-links; expedition map becomes full-bleed with controls overlaid at the bottom.

concepts.site_goal: Present a coastal oceanographic observatory and research institute as a working scientific organization with transparent ongoing research, live sensor data, and an accessible publications and expeditions record for both expert and public audiences.
concepts.description: An institutional oceanography site using deep teal (#11434C) and bone-white (#EEE7D8) with warm rust (#B6532A) accents reserved for live-data indicators and CTAs. The body type is a sturdy scientific serif (Crimson Pro) paired with a condensed grotesk for metadata labels. A left-sidebar navigation groups disciplines under headers (Coastal Dynamics, Benthic Ecology, Acoustics & Bioacoustics, Ocean Chemistry, Climate & Cryosphere) with persistent breadcrumb above content. Pages run at medium-high density with calm, archival typography, scientific figures with formal captions, and a recurring sensor-readings sidebar widget on most pages pulling from the institute's live buoy network.
concepts.motif: A recurring two-line motif: a thin teal hairline above and a dashed teal line below every section header, evoking sonar traces; small rust-colored circles used as live-data dots and active-state markers; figure captions set in italic with a leading 'Fig.' label.

pages.layout_pattern: Left sidebar nav, central column with a hero quote from the director over a coastal photograph, live buoy strip, three featured research threads, and a news rail; right rail with currently-at-sea cruises and upcoming seminars
sections: director's hero statement with coastal photograph
live buoy strip (SST, salinity, wave height, dissolved O2)
three featured research threads with discipline tags

pages.layout_pattern: Discipline-grouped index where each discipline gets a band with a short framing paragraph followed by a 2-column list of labs and shared facilities
sections: shared facilities (Mooring Lab, Isotope Facility, ROV Operations)

pages.layout_pattern: Directory with filter chips above a 3-column card grid; cards show portrait, name, role, lab affiliation, and a one-line research focus; PI cards include an ORCID badge
Two-column profile: left has portrait, contact, ORCID, lab affiliation, education; right has bio, current projects, selected publications with toggle for full list, and a small map of field sites
sections: profile sidebar with portrait and identifiers

pages.layout_pattern: Search-first page with prominent query bar, faceted filter rail, reverse-chronological citation list with expandable abstracts, and bulk export controls at the top of results
sections: search bar with year and author filters
faceted filters (discipline, lab, journal, has dataset, open access)
recently cited by external press callout

pages.layout_pattern: Top half is a regional map showing past and active cruise tracks with status pills; bottom half is a sortable expedition table; a detail drawer slides in when a row or pin is selected
sections: filter: status (planned, at sea, returned), year, region, vessel
detail drawer: science party, daily log timeline, dataset links

pages.layout_pattern: Catalog page with left discipline sidebar, top variable and platform filters, and a card list with sparkline or spectrogram previews and a preview drawer
sections: catalog filters (variable, platform, time range, region)
dataset card list with inline previews
data access guidance (licensing, citation, API endpoints)

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial layout with a lead feature, a two-column secondary story strip, and a chronological archive list below; sidebar contains press contacts and download links for the media kit
sections: press contacts and media kit sidebar

pages.layout_pattern: Single-column listing under role categories with deadline-prominent posting cards, plus an institutional culture section and benefits summary
sections: role category navigation (faculty, postdoc, technical, marine ops, admin)
open positions cards with deadlines and locations
working at the observatory (culture, housing, location)

message_intent: communicate that the observatory runs continuous, sensor-driven research, not occasional studies
make publications and datasets easy to cite and reuse
humanize the scientists through detailed PI pages and field dispatches
support press with a clear newsroom and contacts
content_model: Lab/Group (discipline, lead, focus statement, instruments, partners)
Person (name, role, lab, bio, education, selected pubs, ORCID, field photo)
Publication (DOI, authors, year, journal, abstract, related dataset, related cruise)
Expedition (name, vessel, region, leg dates, science party, status, daily log entries, track)
Dataset (id, type: timeseries/profile/imagery/acoustic, sensors, license, files)
Sensor station (station id, location, depth, variables, last reading timestamp)
News story (date, category, byline, body, related links)
interactions: Left sidebar with collapsible discipline groups and a 'currently at sea' badge that updates from the expeditions API
Live buoy strip across the home and data pages showing SST, salinity, wave height with a 'last updated' timestamp
Publication search with faceted filters and a 'show only with open data' toggle; results have inline BibTeX, RIS, and copy-DOI buttons
Expedition page with an interactive cruise track map, a daily log timeline scrubber, and a science-party roster popover
Dataset preview drawer that opens a small plot (timeseries line or depth profile) without leaving the catalog
Sonar-style audio player on bioacoustics dataset entries with a scrubbable spectrogram
asset_needs: Sonar trace and waveform SVG patterns
Photographs of buoys, gliders, and research vessels at sea
Coastal basemap tiles with bathymetric overlay
Portraits of PIs in field settings
concepts.mobile_behavior: Left sidebar collapses into a slide-in panel triggered from a top bar that retains the discipline-group structure; live buoy strip becomes a horizontally scrollable row of sensor cards; expedition map and timeline stack vertically with the scrubber pinned below the map.
